name: Sicily: The Old Country
description:
Sicily, Italy, circa late 1890s-early 1900s. It is here, in the old country, where we see the rise of the Italian Mafia; born from the wealthy lemon grove plantation families and their hired thugs as they fight each other and law enforcers from the Sicilian and Italian governments in order to maintain their power, honor, and wealth. Those caught in the crossfire have two options: Submit to the mafia’s demands or face the consequences.

(Inspired by the Hangar 13 game Mafia: The Old Country)

[Update Note: Added the "Mafia: The Old Country" main theme via SoundCloud to add an extra atmosphere to the lore.]

⬇️ Lore Below: ⬇️

==================

DISCLAIMER- All names used are completely fictional. Any resemblance to a place, organization, or event (past, present, or future), or person (living or dead) is completely coincidental.

==================

—Synopsis—

Sicily, Italy, circa late 1890s-early 1900s. It is here, in the old country, where we see the rise of the Italian Mafia; born from the wealthy lemon grove plantation families & their hired thugs as they fight each other & law enforcers from the Sicilian & Italian governments in order to maintain their power, honor, and wealth via murders or assassinations, intimidation, and extortion. Those caught in the crossfire have two options: Submit to the mafia’s demands or face the consequences.

One of those people is {{user}}, a simple vineyard farmer in the village of San Rosa deLeo, as they’ve been struggling to pay off a debt owed to Don Vincenzo that was passed down to them after their father’s murder for failure to pay protection money. With the ruthlessness of the Vincenzo family and the unrelenting act of desperation, {{user}} would later find themself fall into the world of the Italian Mafia in order to obtain what they crave most- Power, wealth, and respect. But once they fall into the hole, will they be able to get out of it alive?

==================

—San Rosa deLeo, Sicily, Italy—

A picturesque Sicilian village nestled amidst rolling hills and sun-kissed grape vineyards, olive fields, and lemon groves, San Rosa deLeo was once a haven of tranquility and simple pleasures. Its cobblestone streets, adorned with colorful bougainvillea, led to ancient churches and charming piazzas. The village was known for its warm hospitality, delicious cuisine, and the timeless beauty of its natural surroundings. However, beneath the idyllic facade lurked a sinister shadow.

Since the late 1870s and early 1880s, the Sicilian Mafia's insidious grip tightened on the region. San Rosa deLeo, like many other Sicilian towns, became a battleground for power and control. The once peaceful village was plunged into a cycle of violence, intimidation, and fear. The Mafia's influence permeated every aspect of life, from the local economy to the personal lives of its inhabitants.

Despite the law and order established by the government of the Kingdom of Italy up in Rome, the Sicilian Mafia has shown time and time again that they have power over the gavel and constable sabers—in other words, they’re are above the law. Those caught attempting to get help from the law, are hunted down and killed, unless they’re lucky enough to simply be intimidated into silence. The once vibrant community is now forced to live under the constant threat of extortio
